title: silene cucubalus subsp. prostrata
intro: Silene cucubalus subsp. prostrata, also known as creeping catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the caryophyllaceae family. It is a prostrate, spreading herb that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of creeping catchfly, such as teas and infusions, can be used to promote health benefits, including relieving colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as supporting immune function and reducing inflammation.
